title: Machine Child
anchor_summary: A single-player daughter-raising life sim focused on nurturing choices, scheduling activities, affection growth, dress-up, branching story events, and multiple life-path endings.

similar_games.title: Long Live the Queen
similar_games.why_similar: Choice- and stat-driven raising sim with weekly planning, event outcomes, and multiple endings based on how you develop the protagonist.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently identify Machine Child as a non-combat girl-raising/nurturing simulation. The gameplay loop centers on guiding a daughter through life in a town via 700+ story events, choosing activities, outfits, and developmental paths, building affection, and reaching 22 different endings. That strongly fits life_sim with branching, relationship-driven progression and replayable single-player outcomes. Visual-novel adjacency is present through authored event scenes and multiple endings, but the core identity is life simulation rather than pure VN.

title: Sigma Star Saga DX
anchor_summary: A retro hybrid that mixes top-down action-RPG planet exploration, ability-gated progression, and frequent side-scrolling shmup battles with heavy weapon customization and a campaign-driven sci-fi story.

similar_games.title: Sigma Star Saga
similar_games.why_similar: The original release; same core loop of top-down exploration, random shmup encounters, Gun Data customization, and branching sci-fi campaign.

similar_games.title: The Guardian Legend
similar_games.why_similar: One of the clearest action-adventure/shooter hybrids, combining overhead exploration with separate scrolling shooter combat segments.

similar_games.title: The Knight Witch
similar_games.why_similar: Another explicit shmup/action-adventure mashup with exploration, progression upgrades, and bullet-heavy combat driving the campaign.

similar_games.title: NieR: Automata
similar_games.why_similar: Action-RPG structure that repeatedly shifts into shoot-'em-up and bullet-hell sequences while maintaining story progression and character growth.

similar_games.title: CrossCode
similar_games.why_similar: Top-down sci-fi action-RPG with tool-based exploration, quest progression, puzzle gating, and boss-focused advancement, even without the dedicated shmup layer.

payload.anchor_summary: A retro hybrid that mixes top-down action-RPG planet exploration, ability-gated progression, and frequent side-scrolling shmup battles with heavy weapon customization and a campaign-driven sci-fi story.

similar_games.title: Sigma Star Saga
similar_games.why_similar: The original release; same core loop of top-down exploration, random shmup encounters, Gun Data customization, and branching sci-fi campaign.

similar_games.title: CrossCode
similar_games.why_similar: Top-down sci-fi action-RPG with tool-based exploration, quest progression, puzzle gating, and boss-focused advancement, even without the dedicated shmup layer.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently identify Sigma Star Saga DX as a deliberate hybrid: top-down planetary RPG-style exploration combined with side-scrolling shoot-'em-up space battles, with branching story, multiple endings, boss battles, tool-based exploration, and weapon customization via Gun Data. While the evidence is strong for the game's mixed identity, the available Similar Games Taxonomy V2 archetypes do not cleanly cover this exploration-plus-shmup structure. Candidate RPG fits in the computed taxonomy are incomplete and rely on weak or mismatched assumptions about combat structure and perspective. Because its core gameplay loop materially spans an unsupported genre combination rather than aligning strongly to a single allowed archetype, rejection is more appropriate than forcing an inaccurate classification.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: The original release; same core loop of top-down exploration, random shmup encounters, Gun Data customization, and branching sci-fi campaign.
One of the clearest action-adventure/shooter hybrids, combining overhead exploration with separate scrolling shooter combat segments.
Another explicit shmup/action-adventure mashup with exploration, progression upgrades, and bullet-heavy combat driving the campaign.
Action-RPG structure that repeatedly shifts into shoot-'em-up and bullet-hell sequences while maintaining story progression and character growth.
Top-down sci-fi action-RPG with tool-based exploration, quest progression, puzzle gating, and boss-focused advancement, even without the dedicated shmup layer.

anchor_summary: Cozy single-player restaurant sim centered on hands-on cooking actions, fulfilling customer orders, upgrading and decorating your shop, unlocking recipes/tools, and mixing management progression with character relationships and cooking competitions.

similar_games.why_similar: Order-driven restaurant sim where you prep dishes to customer requests, earn money, and upgrade your shop and ingredients over time.

similar_games.title: Lemon Cake
similar_games.why_similar: Cozy food-service management with hands-on prep, serving customers, upgrading equipment, and customizing your bakery space.

similar_games.title: Touhou Mystia's Izakaya
similar_games.why_similar: Restaurant management blended with character relationships, menu expansion, ingredient gathering, and serving dishes tailored to patrons' tastes.

similar_games.title: Chef Life: A Restaurant Simulator
similar_games.why_similar: More simulation-heavy restaurant management focused on recipe execution, kitchen upgrades, staff/shop progression, and building culinary reputation.

similar_games.title: PlateUp!
similar_games.why_similar: Cooking-and-service loop with restaurant upgrades and layout customization, though it leans more into systemic efficiency and replayable runs.

payload.anchor_summary: Cozy single-player restaurant sim centered on hands-on cooking actions, fulfilling customer orders, upgrading and decorating your shop, unlocking recipes/tools, and mixing management progression with character relationships and cooking competitions.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently present a restaurant-management/cooking simulation loop: inherit and restore a family restaurant, take customer orders, prepare meals through tactile cooking actions, upgrade tools and ingredients, customize the shop, and earn gameplay benefits from decorations. Progression is driven by improving the restaurant and building friendship levels with town residents, with a light authored story around rivals and family legacy. Although 'Meatball Brawls' are framed competitively, they are still culinary scoring duels rather than combat, so the core identity remains management sim/tycoon rather than action. Evidence is strong enough to accept management_tycoon, with some life-sim adjacency from relationship-building and town social elements.

title: People of Note
anchor_summary: Turn-based story JRPG with party recruitment and management, authored single-player campaign structure, and timing-based interactive battle inputs framed as musical performances.

similar_games.title: Rhapsody: A Musical Adventure
similar_games.why_similar: Musical-themed JRPG with an authored story, whimsical worldbuilding, party-based turn-based battles, and songs woven directly into the adventure.

similar_games.title: Super Mario RPG
similar_games.why_similar: Classic turn-based RPG built around timed button presses to enhance attacks and defense, matching the interactive-input battle feel.

similar_games.title: Paper Mario: The Thousand-Year Door
similar_games.why_similar: Party-based turn-based RPG where timing-based action commands make each combat round more active and performance-driven.

similar_games.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
similar_games.why_similar: Modern party RPG that blends turn-based structure with real-time inputs and reactions, emphasizing active participation during battles.

similar_games.title: Persona 5 Royal
similar_games.why_similar: Stylized, authored single-player JRPG focused on party building, character progression, dungeon runs, and a strongly thematic presentation.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored Steam and Metacritic descriptions consistently identify People of Note as a turn-based RPG centered on recruiting a band of party members, traveling across multiple themed regions, and progressing through a story campaign. The combat loop is explicitly party-based and tactical, with timing-based rhythm inputs layered onto turn-based encounters via musical mashup attacks and changing combat conditions. This supports a JRPG-story-RPG identity first, with tactical RPG elements secondary. The rhythm component appears additive to the core party-managed campaign RPG structure rather than defining the game as a standalone rhythm genre.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Musical-themed JRPG with an authored story, whimsical worldbuilding, party-based turn-based battles, and songs woven directly into the adventure.

title: Hidden around the World
anchor_summary: A cozy hidden-object game built around searching hand-drawn scenes, completing light missions, unlocking new locations, and adding a gentle decoration/customization layer, with an especially close fit to other relaxed hidden-object titles that use illustrated diorama scenes and sometimes level sharing.

similar_games.title: Hidden in My Paradise
similar_games.why_similar: Same developer and very close hidden-object loop: search detailed diorama-style scenes, complete relaxed objectives, and engage with light decoration/customization.

similar_games.title: Hidden Through Time
similar_games.why_similar: Relaxed hidden-object play across illustrated scenes with level progression and a strong user-created level sharing component.

similar_games.title: Hidden Through Time 2: Myths & Magic
similar_games.why_similar: Builds on the same cozy hidden-scene formula with more elaborate handcrafted environments and level creation/sharing features.

similar_games.title: Hidden Folks
similar_games.why_similar: One of the clearest genre comps for scanning busy hand-drawn scenes to find specific objects and characters at a calm pace.

similar_games.title: Please, Touch The Artwork 2
similar_games.why_similar: A similarly cozy seek-and-find experience in illustrated scenes, emphasizing relaxed exploration and object discovery over challenge or action.

Metacritic also explicitly labels the genre as Hidden Object, reinforcing hidden_object_puzzle as the best fit.

title: A Rat's Quest: The Way Back Home - Season 1
anchor_summary: Single-player cinematic action-adventure centered on a tiny animal protagonist navigating oversized spaces with climbing, crawling, sneaking, combat, bosses, and perspective shifts between side-on and top-down traversal.

similar_games.title: Ghost of a Tale
similar_games.why_similar: Stealth-forward action-adventure with a small rodent hero, light combat, crawling/sneaking through dangerous spaces, and a strong story focus.

similar_games.title: Moss
similar_games.why_similar: Narrative-driven action-adventure starring a small mouse, mixing traversal, combat, and environmental navigation from a miniature perspective.

similar_games.title: Little Nightmares II
similar_games.why_similar: Cinematic platforming and stealth through oversized hostile environments, emphasizing climbing, squeezing through spaces, and tension-filled encounters.

similar_games.title: Tails of Iron
similar_games.why_similar: Rat-led side-scrolling action-adventure with dangerous creature battles, boss fights, and a darker storybook tone, though more combat-heavy.

similar_games.title: The Spirit and the Mouse
similar_games.why_similar: Small-animal adventure built around exploring human-scale spaces from a tiny perspective, with light traversal and narrative emphasis over heavy combat.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored Steam and Metacritic copy consistently describe a single-player, narrative-driven action adventure centered on movement and navigation: 'climb, jump, crawl, slither, and sneak,' with explicit 'hybrid gameplay' switching between side and top-down perspectives. Combat is clearly present but not dominant over traversal, with a sword, throwing daggers, survival against threats, and 'challenging boss encounters.' The game is framed as a cinematic authored journey with voiced cutscenes, supporting a long-form linear campaign. While the cinematic framing is strong, the core playable identity in the evidence is platform-navigation-led action with stealth elements, making action_platformer the best fit.

title: All Will Fall
anchor_summary: A single-player survival city builder/colony sim focused on real-time base growth, resource chains, faction needs, scenario-based challenges, exploration, and a distinctive physics-driven vertical construction layer in an oceanic post-apocalypse.

similar_games.title: Frostpunk
similar_games.why_similar: Scenario-driven survival city building with harsh resource tradeoffs, population needs, political decisions, and constant pressure management.

similar_games.title: Timberborn
similar_games.why_similar: Systems-heavy colony/city builder with vertical construction, infrastructure planning, production chains, and environmental survival constraints.

similar_games.title: Surviving the Aftermath
similar_games.why_similar: Post-apocalyptic survival colony management with research, resource chains, expeditions, and balancing settlement growth against recurring crises.

similar_games.title: IXION
similar_games.why_similar: Pressure-heavy colony/city management built around layered infrastructure, workforce needs, exploration, and keeping a fragile habitat operational.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently identify All Will Fall as a 'survival city builder/colony sim' centered on physics-based 3D construction, resource management, logistics, production chains, societal needs, research progression, random events, and sandbox/scenario play. The core loop is building and optimizing a settlement rather than commanding armies or pursuing direct combat. Overhead strategy presentation, base/colony growth, systemic simulation, and optimization pressure strongly support city_builder as the primary archetype, with colony_sim and factory_builder as secondary overlaps due faction needs, survival management, and supply-chain emphasis.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Scenario-driven survival city building with harsh resource tradeoffs, population needs, political decisions, and constant pressure management.

title: SHINOBI: Art of Vengeance - SEGA Villains DLC
anchor_summary: Add-on content for a 2D action-platformer built around ninja melee/ranged combat, combo-driven encounters, stage-based progression, traversal challenges, hidden paths, and boss-focused side content. The clearest neighbor is the base game it extends, followed by recognizable ninja/action-platformers with similar precision combat and platforming flow.

similar_games.title: Shinobi: Art of Vengeance
similar_games.why_similar: The DLC directly extends the base game's 2D ninja action-platforming, combat toolkit, stage structure, and progression systems.

similar_games.title: The Messenger
similar_games.why_similar: Fast 2D ninja platforming with precise movement, enemy-heavy stages, and combat-driven traversal.

similar_games.title: Strider
similar_games.why_similar: High-speed side-scrolling action centered on acrobatic swordplay, mobility, and traversal through combat spaces.

similar_games.title: Cyber Shadow
similar_games.why_similar: Retro-style ninja action-platformer emphasizing tough combat sequences, movement precision, and boss encounters.

similar_games.title: Mark of the Ninja: Remastered
similar_games.why_similar: Another ninja-focused 2D side-scroller with gadgets and traversal, though more stealth-leaning than combo-action focused.

The clearest neighbor is the base game it extends, followed by recognizable ninja/action-platformers with similar precision combat and platforming flow.

similar_games.title: Shinobi: Art of Vengeance

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Accepted via DLC inheritance from the parent game because the DLC is clearly additive rather than mode-shifting: it adds 5 stages, 3 bosses, ninpos, skins, music, and boss rush modes. The parent game's stored description explicitly defines the core gameplay as 'an all-new 2D action platformer' with precision ninja combat, combos, obstacle-overcoming tools, challenging platforming puzzles, hidden secrets, and multiple stages. That strongly supports action_platformer with side-scrolling 2D stage-based progression, dominant combat, platform traversal, boss encounters, and precision platforming. The DLC's boss-rush/stage content reinforces rather than contradicts that identity.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: The DLC directly extends the base game's 2D ninja action-platforming, combat toolkit, stage structure, and progression systems.

title: Cursed Words: The Word Game That Isn't
anchor_summary: A single-player word-building roguelike where each run revolves around making words, stacking rule-breaking upgrades and modifiers, clearing staged encounters and bosses, and pushing score through strong synergies.

similar_games.title: Balatro
similar_games.why_similar: Run-based, highly replayable synergy builder built around breaking a familiar ruleset for huge score combos, with escalating blinds/boss-like run structure.

similar_games.title: Letter Quest: Grimm's Journey Remastered
similar_games.why_similar: Word construction is directly tied to combat, upgrades, and enemy encounters, making it one of the clearest word-game-meets-run-based-progression comparisons.

similar_games.title: Bookworm Adventures Deluxe
similar_games.why_similar: A classic word-combat game where building better words drives battles, item usage, and tactical enemy clearing.

similar_games.title: SpellTower+
similar_games.why_similar: Shares the core loop of forming words from a constrained board while managing escalating board states and score pressure, though it is less run-roguelike.

similar_games.title: Boggle
similar_games.why_similar: Cursed Words explicitly starts from a Boggle-like word-finding foundation before layering on roguelike modifiers and progression.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently identify Cursed Words as a roguelike word/strategy game built around repeated runs, stage-based progression, synergy-building upgrades, and rule-bending modifiers. The Steam detailed description gives concrete loop evidence: each run has five stages with encounters and a boss, victory at Stage 5, challenge tiers afterward, and build-defining systems via Character, Sticker upgrades, Stamp loadout, and consumable Tiles. This strongly supports a strategic word-puzzle identity with roguelite/deck-growth structure rather than an action genre. Combat is metaphorical ('boss encounters') but the gameplay described is non-combat, methodical, and score/synergy focused.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Run-based, highly replayable synergy builder built around breaking a familiar ruleset for huge score combos, with escalating blinds/boss-like run structure.
Word construction is directly tied to combat, upgrades, and enemy encounters, making it one of the clearest word-game-meets-run-based-progression comparisons.
A classic word-combat game where building better words drives battles, item usage, and tactical enemy clearing.
Shares the core loop of forming words from a constrained board while managing escalating board states and score pressure, though it is less run-roguelike.
Cursed Words explicitly starts from a Boggle-like word-finding foundation before layering on roguelike modifiers and progression.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored store descriptions consistently identify the game as a minigolf roguelite: each run is different, levels have their own rules, players spend shots as score, collect coins for multipliers, buy equipment/power-ups, and progress through gates toward higher stages. This is strong evidence for a run-based score-chasing sports/arcade structure with roguelite modifiers. However, the allowed Similar Games Taxonomy V2 archetype list does not contain a fitting minigolf/golf-arcade/roguelite sports archetype. 'sports_sim' is a poor fit because the game is not presented as simulation-focused, and other archetypes are even less appropriate. Rejected due to taxonomy coverage mismatch rather than lack of gameplay evidence.

Steam and Metacritic both support a long-form campaign structure and JRPG identity; the gameplay copy emphasizes party-based strategic combat and story progression, not match-based or fighting-game play. The strongest signals are side-scrolling presentation, dominant melee combat, boss-centric structure, and a single-player campaign loop. The copy emphasizes combos, unlockable skills and charms, and preparing builds before battles, which supports action-platformer progression with some metroidvania/beat-em-up adjacency. However, exploration, backtracking, and discovery are not described strongly enough to make metroidvania the primary archetype, and the boss-focused structure is less typical of a pure beat 'em up.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored evidence consistently identifies the game as a first-person narrative experience about reliving the life of Jesus, exploring Holy Land locations, meeting biblical figures, resisting temptation, reading scripture, praying, and performing miracles. However, the available copy does not establish a clear gameplay structure that maps strongly to any allowed Similar Games Taxonomy V2 archetype. There is no strong evidence for shooter, stealth, survival, RPG, immersive sim, puzzle, platforming, management, tactics, or sports/racing identity. Combat is unclear, progression is unclear, and the phrase 'simulation' appears only as a storefront genre label rather than a described systems loop. This looks closer to a first-person narrative/adventure or walking-sim style experience, which is not represented in the allowed archetype list, so classification would be too speculative.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored evidence consistently supports an arcade-style undead survival action game with solo/local co-op, leaderboard competition, 20 stages, weapons/items, and Survival/Arcade modes. However, the evidence does not clearly establish a supported taxonomy archetype: perspective is unspecified, moment-to-moment structure is too vague to distinguish between beat_em_up, action_horror, co_op_horror, or another action subtype, and the current computed near-misses (party_game, sports_sim) are clearly false positives from sparse metadata. Because the core gameplay identity cannot be mapped confidently to one of the allowed archetypes, rejection is safer than forcing a weak fit.

title: Fishbowl
anchor_summary: Narrative slice-of-life visual novel/adventure centered on grief and early adulthood, with day-by-day choices, remote social interactions, light task/minigame play, and memory-focused storytelling in a cozy surreal frame.

similar_games.title: If Found...
similar_games.why_similar: Intimate coming-of-age interactive narrative about loss and identity, driven by choices and personal relationships more than challenge-heavy gameplay.

similar_games.title: Coffee Talk
similar_games.why_similar: Conversation-led slice-of-life structure with light routine gameplay, cozy presentation, and a focus on supporting others through everyday emotional struggles.

similar_games.title: Mutazione
similar_games.why_similar: Gentle narrative adventure blending daily life, community conversations, and surreal elements while processing family history and emotional healing.

similar_games.title: A Memoir Blue
similar_games.why_similar: Short, emotionally focused interactive story built around memories, grief, and light symbolic interactions rather than complex systems.

similar_games.title: Unpacking
similar_games.why_similar: Quiet domestic storytelling through everyday tasks and object-based memory reconstruction, echoing Fishbowl’s intimate homebound self-discovery loop.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions repeatedly and explicitly call Fishbowl a 'visual novel' and a narrative slice-of-life coming-of-age story. The core loop centers on making choices over the course of a month, video-calling friends/family/coworkers, doing daily care/work tasks, and sorting through belongings and memory-related puzzles. The text stresses 'no bad endings, just different paths' and says 'the choices you make will shape the possibilities in Alo's future,' which strongly supports authored branching and dialogue/relationship-focused interaction. No combat is described anywhere; instead the gameplay is framed as intimate, cozy, grief-focused narrative progression with light puzzle/matching activities. That combination fits visual_novel much better than broader adventure labels.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Intimate coming-of-age interactive narrative about loss and identity, driven by choices and personal relationships more than challenge-heavy gameplay.
Conversation-led slice-of-life structure with light routine gameplay, cozy presentation, and a focus on supporting others through everyday emotional struggles.
Gentle narrative adventure blending daily life, community conversations, and surreal elements while processing family history and emotional healing.
Short, emotionally focused interactive story built around memories, grief, and light symbolic interactions rather than complex systems.
Quiet domestic storytelling through everyday tasks and object-based memory reconstruction, echoing Fishbowl’s intimate homebound self-discovery loop.

title: Clean Up Earth
anchor_summary: A relaxing cleanup-and-restoration game built around vacuuming waste, recycling materials, rebuilding areas, and watching environments visibly recover, with solo play plus co-op/community multiplayer as a major differentiator.

similar_games.title: Fresh Start Cleaning Simulator
similar_games.why_similar: Directly centered on cleaning polluted spaces and restoring nature with immediate visual transformation.

similar_games.title: PowerWash Simulator
similar_games.why_similar: Shares the satisfying cleanup loop, tool-based environmental interaction, and relaxing progression through visibly restored spaces.

similar_games.title: No Place Like Home
similar_games.why_similar: Combines vacuuming trash, recycling resources, exploration, and rebuilding a cleaner world in a light, accessible loop.

similar_games.title: Loddlenaut
similar_games.why_similar: Focuses on cleaning pollution and reviving ecosystems, with a calm restoration-first structure and visible environmental recovery.

similar_games.title: The Gunk
similar_games.why_similar: Uses suction-based cleanup and exploration to heal corrupted environments, though it leans more toward guided action-adventure than sandbox co-op restoration.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored evidence consistently describes a relaxing environmental restoration/cleanup game focused on vacuuming waste, recycling materials, rebuilding damaged structures, exploring large maps, and cooperating with up to 25 players. The strongest supported traits are multiplayer/co-op support, large shared spaces, casual/simulation framing, and non-combat restorative play. However, the Similar Games Taxonomy V2 archetype list does not contain a suitable home for a cooperative cleanup/restoration exploration sim with minimal or no combat. The closest available archetypes are poor fits because they require identity-defining elements not supported here, such as farming/life loops, factory/engineering systems, colony/city management, survival pressures, shooter combat, or RPG progression. Because the gameplay identity is clear but falls outside the available archetype set, the title should be rejected rather than forced into an inaccurate category.
